info.name: gong-entrypoints — callschedulers
info.description: Triggers for the callschedulers module entry points, exposed by the gong-entrypoints app. Requests are grouped into a folder per entrypoint. Set the active Postman Environment (callschedulers.localhost) to point {{baseUrl}} at the running gong-entrypoints app (always localhost:8080). The downstream CallScheduler must be running locally on port 8091 (IntelliJ 'CallScheduler' run config).

item.name: send-calendar-event
item.description: Entrypoint #2 — send a calendar event through the core scheduling pipeline. Submits a CallSchedulingRequest (type CALENDAR_EVENT) to the troubleshooter, which puts it on the CALL_SCHEDULING_REQUESTS Kafka topic. The consumer drives the full pipeline: distributed lock → callSchedulingRequestsService.callIncomingCalendarInviteHandler() → validation → scheduling decision. Breakpoint: CallSchedulingRequestsConsumer.accept() line 87. callCreationMechanism=CALENDAR_SYNC is the standard non-email calendar-sync path (no opt-in email side-effects). companyId=9001, userId=501, emailAddress=alice@acme-corp.test (from seed-dialers-local.sql in gong-telephony-systems/dev/).

item.name: A — CALENDAR_EVENT, future meeting (happy path smoke test)

body.raw:
{
  "companyId": 9001,
  "callSchedulingEventType": "CALENDAR_EVENT",
  "callCreationMechanism": "CALENDAR_SYNC",
  "calendarPayload": {
    "userId": 501,
    "emailAddress": "alice@acme-corp.test",
    "provider": "google",
    "providerEventId": "smoke-test-event-001",
    "iCalUID": "smoke-test-event-001@google.com",
    "recurringEventId": null,
    "etag": "1234567890",
    "organizer": {
      "name": "Alice",
      "emailAddress": "alice@acme-corp.test",
      "responseStatus": "ACCEPTED",
      "role": "ORGANIZER"
    },
    "creator": null,
    "invitees": [
      {
        "name": "Bob",
        "emailAddress": "bob@acme-corp.test",
        "responseStatus": "ACCEPTED",
        "role": "PARTICIPANT"
      }
    ],
    "startTime": "2026-08-01T10:00:00Z",
    "endTime": "2026-08-01T11:00:00Z",
    "createTime": "2026-07-01T09:00:00Z",
    "lastModifiedTime": "2026-07-01T09:00:00Z",
    "originalStartTime": null,
    "summary": "Smoke Test Meeting",
    "description": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "location": null,
    "additionalMeetingUrls": [],
    "isPrivateOrConfidential": false,
    "isAllDay": false,
    "isCancelled": false,
    "isRecurrent": false,
    "isCrmIntegrationEnabled": false,
    "isMeetingIndexed": false
  }
}

request.description: Path A — standard CALENDAR_SYNC event, future meeting, non-recurring. The event goes onto the Kafka topic and the consumer drives it through the full scheduling pipeline. Expect 200, body 'Done'. companyId=9001 (Acme Corp), userId=501 (Alice), emailAddress=alice@acme-corp.test — from seed-dialers-local.sql. The Zoom URL in description exercises the meeting-URL extraction path.

request.description: Path B — same iCalUID as path A but isCancelled=true. Exercises the CalendarCancelEvent branch in toCalendarEvent() and the cancellation leg of the scheduling pipeline. Run path A first to create the scheduled call, then this to cancel it. Expect 200, body 'Done'.

request.description: Fires the calendar event N times (5s apart). Uses the path-A body so every iteration is a clean schedule attempt.

item.name: cancel-blacklisted-calls
item.description: Entrypoint #1 — cancel blacklisted calls for a company. Cancels any scheduled call recordings whose participants are on the company's blacklist. Useful for verifying blacklist enforcement end-to-end without waiting for the next scheduled task run. Downstream: PUT /troubleshooting/blacklistedCalls/cancelBlacklistedForCompany on CallScheduler (TroubleshootingBlacklistedCalls.cancelBlacklistedForCompany).

query.description: The company whose blacklisted scheduled calls should be cancelled.

request.description: Cancels blacklisted calls targeting localhost:8091. Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled blacklisted calls for companyId=9001'.

query.description: The company whose blacklisted scheduled calls should be cancelled.

request.description: Same as local but targets callschedulers.hybrid-url (callscheduler.modules.terry-collins-dev-env.c1-devex.ilc1.internal.gongio.net). Requires VPN. Set a breakpoint in TroubleshootingBlacklistedCalls.cancelBlacklistedForCompany() in the hybrid CallScheduler.

item.name: cancel-call
item.description: Entrypoint #3 — cancel a specific scheduled call by callId. Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.cancelScheduledCallByOwner() → CancelCallService.cancelByOwnerScheduledCall(). Use after send-calendar-event to get a callId, then cancel it. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/cancelScheduledCallByOwner.

query.description: ID from scheduled_calls table. Run send-calendar-event first to create one.

request.description: Cancels a specific scheduled call. Get the callId from the scheduled_calls table after running send-calendar-event. Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled callId=<id> for companyId=9001'.
Same as local but targets the hybrid CallScheduler. Requires VPN.

item.name: restore-call
item.description: Entrypoint #4 — restore a previously cancelled scheduled call. Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.restoreCancelledCallByOwner() → RestoreCancelledCallService. Use after cancel-call to test the cancel → restore round-trip. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/restoreCancelledCallByOwner.

query.description: ID of a previously cancelled call. Run cancel-call first.

request.description: Restores a cancelled scheduled call. Requires running send-calendar-event then cancel-call first. Expect 200 with body 'Done — restored callId=<id> for companyId=9001'.
Same as local but targets the hybrid CallScheduler. Requires VPN.

item.name: cancel-internal-meetings
item.description: Entrypoint #5 — cancel all internal meeting recordings for a company. Exercises ScheduledCallsActionsController.cancelScheduledInternalMeetingsCallsRecordings() → CancelCallService. Simulates a company admin disabling internal meeting recording. Downstream: POST /scheduledCallsActions/cancelScheduledInternalMeetingsCallsRecordings.

request.description: Cancels all internal meeting scheduled calls for company 9001. Expect 200 with body 'Done — cancelled internal meeting recordings for companyId=9001'. No-op if no internal meetings are scheduled.
Same as local but targets the hybrid CallScheduler. Requires VPN.

item.name: cancel-inactive-users
item.description: Entrypoint #6 — cancel inactive users' scheduled calls and rescan co-invitees' calendars. The offboarding cleanup path. Exercises TroubleshootingInactiveUsersCalls.cancelInactiveUsersCallsAndRescanOtherCalendarsForCompany(). Only CALENDAR_INGESTER mechanism calls are affected. Downstream: POST /troubleshooting/inactive-users-calls/cancel-inactive-users-calls-and-rescan-other-calendars-for-company.

query.description: Users inactive for this many days are treated as inactive. Default: 7.

request.description: Cancels calls owned by users inactive ≥7 days, then rescans co-invitees. Expect 200. No-op if no matching inactive users are found.
Same as local but targets the hybrid CallScheduler. Requires VPN.
